---
title: "Lecture 10: CSS Box Model & Positioning"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "February 10, 2026"
code-annotations: below
---


## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Lecture 10]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[CSS Box Model & Positioning]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Week 3 | Unit II: CSS Styling & JavaScript<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Learning Objectives"}

[Learning Objectives]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Master the CSS box model concept
- Understand margin, padding, and borders
- Learn different positioning methods
- Create complex layouts with positioning
- Implement z-index layering
:::

::: {.notes}
The box model is fundamental to CSS layout. Understanding it is essential for controlling spacing and positioning of elements.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Box Model Concept"}

[The CSS Box Model]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Every HTML element is a box with layers:

```
+-------------------------------------+
|     Margin (outside)                |
|  +-------------------------------+  |
|  |  Border                       |  |
|  |  +-------------------------+  |  |
|  |  |  Padding (inside)       |  |  |
|  |  |  +-------------------+  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  Content          |  |  |  |
|  |  |  +-------------------+  |  |  |
|  |  +-------------------------+  |  |
|  +-------------------------------+  |
+-------------------------------------+
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Content & Width"}

[Content Area & Width]{.slide-title}

<hr>

The `width` and `height` properties control content size:

```{.css code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4"}
div { width: 300px; }      /* Content width only */
div { height: 200px; }     /* Content height only */
div { box-sizing: border-box; } /* Include border/padding */
```

**Box-sizing modes:**

```css
/* Default: width = only content */
box-sizing: content-box;

/* Include padding and border in width */
box-sizing: border-box;
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .center-text .topbr}
[Use `box-sizing: border-box;` for easier calculations!]{.magenta-text}
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Padding"}

[Padding (Internal Spacing)]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Space inside the box, around content:

```{.css code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4"}
padding: 20px;              /* All 4 sides */
padding: 10px 20px;         /* Top/bottom, left/right */
padding: 10px 20px 30px 40px; /* T, R, B, L */
padding-top: 10px;          /* Individual sides */
```

**Visual example:**
```
+-------------------------------+
|      Content (text)           |
|     with 20px padding         |
+-------------------------------+
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Margin"}

[Margin (External Spacing)]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Space outside the box, between elements:

```{.css code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4"}
margin: 20px;               /* All 4 sides */
margin: 10px 20px;          /* Top/bottom, left/right */
margin: 10px 20px 30px 40px; /* T, R, B, L */
margin-bottom: 30px;        /* Individual sides */
```

**Key difference from padding:**
- Padding = inside (pushes content inward)
- Margin = outside (pushes other elements away)

---

## {data-menu-title="Border"}

[Border Styling]{.slide-title}

<hr>

The border goes around padding:

```{.css code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4"}
border: 2px solid black;           /* Width, style, color */
border: 1px dashed red;            /* Dashed borders */
border-radius: 10px;               /* Rounded corners */
border-top: 3px solid blue;        /* Individual sides */
```

**Border styles:**
```css
solid, dashed, dotted, double, groove, ridge, inset, outset
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Position Property"}

[CSS Position Property]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Controls how elements are positioned:

::: {.body-text-m}
::: {.fragment}
[**static**]{.teal-text} - Default, flows with document
:::
::: {.fragment}
[**relative**]{.teal-text} - Move relative to original position
:::
::: {.fragment}
[**absolute**]{.teal-text} - Position relative to parent
:::
::: {.fragment}
[**fixed**]{.teal-text} - Fixed to viewport (doesn't scroll)
:::
::: {.fragment}
[**sticky**]{.teal-text} - Sticks when scrolled past
:::
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Static Positioning"}

[Position: Static (Default)]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Elements follow normal document flow:

```{.css code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|}
div { position: static; } /* Default - no positioning */

/* top, bottom, left, right have NO effect */
div { position: static; top: 20px; } /* Ignored */
```

**Visual:**
```
+-------+
| Box 1 |
+-------+
+-------+
| Box 2 |  <- Normal flow, boxes stack
+-------+
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Relative Positioning"}

[Position: Relative]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Move element relative to its normal position:

```{.css code-line-numbers="|1-5"}
div {
    position: relative;
    top: 20px;      /* Move down 20px from original */
    left: 10px;     /* Move right 10px from original */
}
```

**Key point:** Other elements don't fill the gap!

```
Box 1 original position: +-------+
Box 1 after relative:       +-------+  <- Moved, but space remains
+-------+
| Box 2 |  <- Stays in original spot
+-------+
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Absolute Positioning"}

[Position: Absolute]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Remove from document flow, position relative to parent:

```html
<!-- Parent must have position set -->
<div style="position: relative;">
    <div style="position: absolute; top: 10px; left: 20px;">
        Absolutely positioned
    </div>
</div>
```

**Properties:**
```css
top, bottom, left, right /* Distance from parent edges */
```

:::  {.fragment .body-text-m .center-text .topbr}
[Other elements ignore absolute elements (flows under them)]{.teal-text}
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Fixed Positioning"}

[Position: Fixed]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Position relative to viewport (doesn't scroll):

```{.css code-line-numbers="|1-4"}
.navbar {
    position: fixed;
    top: 0;
    width: 100%;
}
```

**Perfect for:**
- Navigation bars that stay at top
- Sticky sidebars
- "Back to top" buttons

::: {.fragment}
```css
.chat-widget {
    position: fixed;
    bottom: 20px;
    right: 20px;
}
```
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Z-Index"}

[Z-Index (Stacking Order)]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Control which element appears on top:

```{.css code-line-numbers="|1|2|3"}
.background { z-index: 1; }   /* Below */
.foreground { z-index: 10; }  /* On top */
.modal { z-index: 1000; }     /* Highest */
```

**Visual:**
```
     ^ z-axis (depth)
     |
  10 |     +------------------+
     |     | Foreground      |
   1 | +---+------------------+---+
     | | Background           |
     +---+---------------------+-> x-axis
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .center-text .topbr}
[Higher z-index = appears on top]{.magenta-text}
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Display Property"}

[Display Property Values]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Controls how elements are rendered:

```{.css code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4"}
display: block;      /* Full width, new line */
display: inline;     /* Only needed width, same line */
display: inline-block; /* Inline but accepts width/height */
display: flex;       /* Flexible box layout */
display: grid;       /* Grid layout */
display: none;       /* Hidden, no space taken */
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Practical Example"}

[Layout Example: Navigation & Hero]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.html code-line-numbers="|1-4|5-10"}
<nav style="position: fixed; top: 0; z-index: 100;">
    Navigate links here
</nav>

<section class="hero" style="position: relative; margin-top: 60px;">
    <h1>Welcome</h1>
    <button style="position: absolute; bottom: 20px; right: 20px;">
        Scroll Down
    </button>
</section>
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Resources"}

[Resources & References]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
- [MDN: Box Model](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn/CSS/Building_blocks/The_box_model)
- [MDN: Position](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/position)
- [CSS-Tricks: Position](https://css-tricks.com/almanac/properties/p/position/)
- [Course content: Week 3](/content/week-03.qmd)
:::

---

## {#end-slide data-menu-title="End Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="page-center">
<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Questions?]{.white-text}
</div>
<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Next: Lecture 11 - Bootstrap Framework
</div>
</div>
